deficit_span.speaker: teacher

context_excerpt.text: who says 12 well let s count em out okay we ll say that that s your rectangular prism i m gonna go over the edges with this pink piece of chalk and count them off as i go okay and this is what happens when i ask this question most kids do say eight and the eight that they were talking about is they looked at one two three and four and that would be here one two three and four and then they say i got these ones on the bottom so they would say five six seven and eight and they say eight but they forget something has to connect the top and the bottom right one two three four so there are four more so an eight nine ten 11 12 this question is also fair game on your assessment guys so they ve thrown this question out there i don t suspect that they ll ask you how many faces there are you all know that from your surface area or rectangular prisms but that s a fair fair game question to ask how many edges there are in assessment so we need to be good with that everybody set

scenes.rationale: The teacher attributes the student's answer to inattention and dismisses it as 'just throwing out numbers,' framing the response as a motivational/behavioral deficit rather than describing the error neutrally.
